title: muscari neglectum subsp. atlanticum
intro: Muscari neglectum subsp. atlanticum, also known as the Atlantic grape hyacinth, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Asparagaceae family. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Research suggests that the Atlantic grape hyacinth may offer various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which could help protect against cell damage and diseases such as cancer and Alzheimer's. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where its extracts are believed to contribute to improved cardiovascular health and immune function.
